questions.title: How many Wajibat (obligatory acts) of prayer?
questions.explanation:
There are 14 pillars of prayer which are as follows:

(1) Standing during obligatory prayers

(2) The opening takbeer (saying “Allahu akbar”)

(3) Reciting al-Faatihah

(4) Rukoo’ (bowing),

(5) Rising from bowing

(6) Standing up straight

(7) Sujood (prostration)

(8) Rising from prostration

(9) Sitting between the two prostrations

(10) Being at ease in each of these physical pillars

(11) The final tashahhud

(12) Sitting to recite the final tashahhud and the two salaams

(13) The two salaams. This means saying twice, “al-salaamu ‘alaykum wa rahmat-Allah"

(14) Follow the pillars in the stated order

[Deliberately prostrating before bowing invalidates the prayer; if done by mistake, one must bow and then prostrate.]

questions.title: Who established the foundation of Umayyad dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Umayyads were the first Muslim dynasty, established in 661 in Damascus. Their dynasty succeeded the leadership of the first four caliphs—Abū Bakr, ʿUmar I, ʿUthmān, and ʿAlī. It was established by Muʿāwiyah ibn Abī Sufyān, a native of Mecca and a contemporary of the Prophet Muḥammad.

questions.title: Khalid ibn al-Walid (R.A) embraced Islam in which Hijri?
questions.explanation: Khalid ibn al-Walid was a well-known Arab Muslim commander in the service of the Islamic prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and the caliphs Abu Bakr (R.A) and Umar (R.A). Historians have reported different narrations on the date that Khalid b. Walid converted to Islam. In the year 6 AH or 8 AH Khalid embraced Islam in Muhammad’s presence alongside the Qurayshite Amr ibn al-As. However, according to a famous report, Khalid converted to Islam in the first of Safar 8 AH (c. 629)before the Conquest of Mecca.

questions.title: Battle of Qadisiya was fought against the Persians in ________A.D.
questions.explanation: The Battle of al-Qadisiyyah, fought in 636, was a decisive battle between the Arab Muslim army and the army of the Sasanian Empire of Persia during the first period of Muslim conquests.

questions.title: The first meal in Jannah will be_______?
questions.explanation: It is proven in the saheeh Sunnah that the first food offered to welcome the people of Paradise when they enter it will be “the caudate lobe of whale liver”.

questions.title: Tafseer al Quran al Azeem was written by ______?
questions.explanation: Imam Ibn Kathir on Tawassul: Many have stated this tradition. One of them is Abū Mansūr Sabbagh who writes in his book al-Hikayat-ul-mashhurah that, according to ‘Utbi, once he was sitting beside the Prophet’s grave when a bedouin came and he said, “Peace be on you, O Allah’s Messenger.

questions.title: Takbeer-e-Tashreeq is recited in______?
questions.explanation:
Takbir al-Tashreeq is a prayer recited by pilgrims after every Fardh Salah during Hajj.

Starting immediately after Fajr Salah on the 9th of Dhul Hijjah, it is Wajib for Hajj pilgrims to audibly recite Takbir al-Tashreeq at least once after every Fardh Salah, up to and including Asr Salah on the 13th of Dhul Hijjah (a total of 23 Fardh Salahs).

 

اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ لَا إلَهَ إلَّا اللَّهُ وَاَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْد

questions.title: The period of the reign of Abu Ishaq Muḥammad ibn Harun al-Rashid is from_____?
questions.explanation: Abū Isḥāq Muḥammad ibn Hārūn al-Rashīd, better known by his regnal name al-Muʿtaṣim biʾllāh , was the eighth Abbasid caliph, ruling from 833 until his death in 842.

questions.title: In which Surah every verse ends with the letter ‘Ra’?
questions.explanation: Surah Al-'Asr also ends 'Ra'.

questions.title: How many times Bismillahir Rahmaanir Raheem is repeated in the Holy Quran?
questions.explanation:
Bismillahir Rahmaanir Raheem is repeated in the Holy Quran  114 Times
Every surah in the Quran opens with the statement as the Bismillah, except Surah 9 (Surah Tuba).

Surah Naml, which is the 27th Chapter, has the entire bismillahir rahmanir raheem in the 30th verse.
This is why Surah Naml is known as the Surah with two bismillahs.

questions.title: Who are exempt from Ramadan’s fasting?
questions.explanation: As one of the five pillars, or duties, of Islam, fasting during the month of Ramadan is mandatory for all healthy adult Muslims. Children who have not reached puberty, the elderly, those who are physically or mentally incapable of fasting, pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers and travelers are exempt.

questions.title: Which days are called Ayam-e-Tashreeq?
questions.explanation:
Uqbah bin Amir (R) said: The Messenger of Allahﷺsaid:

"The Day of Arafah, the Day of Nahr, and the Days of Tashriq (11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul-Hijjah) are Eid for us. The people of Islam, and they are days of eating and drinking,"

Jami at-Tirmidhi 773, Sunan Ibn Majah Vol 1: 1720 and Sunan an Nasai 3007

questions.title: What is the name of the forces of Hell (Jahanam)?
questions.explanation:
In Islam the Zabaniyah (الزبانية‎) are the forces of hell, who torment the sinners, also called the Angels of punishment or Guardians of Hell.

They are often identified with the Nineteen Angels of Hell mentioned in Quran 66:6 and 74:30 or as their subordinates.

Malik is the name of the angel of Jahanam [Hell]
